name: planning-with-files
description: Implements Manus-style file-based planning for complex tasks. Creates task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md. Use when starting complex multi-step tasks, research projects, or any task requiring >5 tool calls.
capabilities: Creates task_plan.md for phase tracking and goal management
Creates findings.md to store research and discoveries
Creates progress.md for session logging and test results
Provides templates for consistent planning structure
Includes helper scripts for session initialization and completion checking
Implements the 3-strike error protocol for systematic problem solving
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 101 potential issues but evaluation confirms all are FALSE POSITIVES. The detected external_commands are markdown documentation examples showing AI tool usage, not actual shell execution. Network access is limited to a single legitimate reference URL. Filesystem access is standard file operations. The skill implements Manus-style planning methodology with no malicious intent. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: AI agents lose track of goals during long tasks. This skill uses persistent markdown files as external memory so agents can handle complex multi-step projects without losing context.
limitations: Does not execute code - only creates planning documentation
Requires manual invocation or detection of complex tasks
User must read plan files to maintain context awareness
Files must be created in the working project directory

useCases.targetUser: Developers and researchers doing information gathering
useCases.title: Research Projects
useCases.description: Use task_plan.md to track research phases, findings.md to store discoveries, and progress.md to log session activity. Prevents losing track of sources and insights.

useCases.title: Bug Fixing
useCases.description: Create a task plan with phases for diagnosis, root cause identification, fix implementation, and testing. Track errors encountered to avoid repeating failed approaches.

useCases.title: Feature Development
useCases.description: Plan feature implementation with phases, store design decisions in findings.md, and log progress throughout development. Maintains focus across many implementation steps.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Task Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a task_plan.md file with phases for: [describe your task]. Use the planning-with-files skill structure.

promptTemplates.title: Research with Findings
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up planning files for a research task on [topic]. Create task_plan.md with phases, findings.md for storing sources, and progress.md for logging.

promptTemplates.title: Debug Session
promptTemplates.prompt: Use planning-with-files to debug [issue]. Create task_plan.md with diagnosis phases, track errors in the plan, and document findings as you discover root cause.

promptTemplates.title: Full Project Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the complete planning-with-files workflow: create all three planning files, implement the 2-action rule, use the 3-strike error protocol, and apply read-before-decide pattern.

bestPractices: Create task_plan.md BEFORE starting any complex task with more than 5 tool calls
Re-read the plan file before making major decisions to refresh goals in context
Update task_plan.md after each phase to track progress and mark completion status
antiPatterns: Using TodoWrite for persistence instead of creating task_plan.md files
Starting execution immediately without creating a plan file first
Hiding errors and retrying silently instead of logging to plan file

faq.question: Where should I create the planning files?
faq.answer: Create task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md in your project directory, not in the skill installation folder. This keeps your planning files alongside your code.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use planning-with-files for any task requiring more than 5 tool calls, research projects, multi-step implementations, or anything requiring organization.

faq.question: What is the 2-action rule?
faq.answer: After every 2 view, browser, or search operations, immediately save key findings to files. This prevents visual and multimodal information from being lost.

faq.question: How does the 3-strike error protocol work?
faq.answer: Attempt 1: Diagnose and fix. Attempt 2: Try alternative approach. Attempt 3: Broader rethink. After 3 failures: escalate to user with what you tried.

faq.question: What is the read-before-decide pattern?
faq.answer: Before major decisions, read task_plan.md to bring goals back into your attention window. This prevents losing track of original objectives during long tasks.

faq.question: What is KV-cache and why does it matter?
faq.answer: KV-cache is a mechanism for caching computed tokens. A high hit rate reduces costs significantly (10x cheaper). Stable prompts maintain cache validity.
